title: A Translation From Beyond the Grave
display_title: A Translation From Beyond the Grave
wikitext:
[]: A Translation From Beyond the Grave             Quote Post by NAveryW » Sat Oct 25, 2008 12:11 pm Zuggy asked me to post this on EGF since he was unable to. I'm hoping this is the right subforum... In any case, it's a poem written by Megumi Hayashibara and published in ＥＶＡ友の会. Tom Servo wrote: I have translated the text in the blue box as best I can. From the voice of the cast: written by Megumi Hayashibara The me within myself The you within myself Wanting to know, not wanting to know The me further within myself Wanting to meet, not wanting to meet What is it that limits meeting the me further within myself I'm still weak You're weak as well The weakness of the me within myself The me who's always running away This isn't me When I came to accept that part of myself Others seemed to accept that part of myself as well, possibly Man, mother, other things, sex, Man's peculiar benefit in being man The reason for innocence Is to gain benefit from knowing I will protect you Yet it's not for your benefit It's for mine... Rei: Megumi Hayashibara This is interesting as it throws new light on Rei's "I will protect you" line in episode 6. It shows what Megumi thought Rei's motivations were at that time. If Hideaki had overseen that episode with Megumi in the booth it might by extension show what Hideaki's intentions were in having Rei say that line.

[]:             Quote Post by Zaiku » Sat Oct 25, 2008 4:05 pm Indeed it is interesting stuff. Rei's VA is as much of a poet as she is. ^^

[]:             Quote Post by Otakon 08 Ikari » Sat Oct 25, 2008 4:25 pm I love stuff like this, Eva never stops to amaze me.

[]: Re: A Translation From Beyond the Grave             Quote Post by V » Sat Oct 25, 2008 6:12 pm NAveryW wrote: This is interesting as it throws new light on Rei's "I will protect you" line in episode 6. It shows what Megumi thought Rei's motivations were at that time. If Hideaki had overseen that episode with Megumi in the booth it might by extension show what Hideaki's intentions were in having Rei say that line.

[]:             Quote Post by Lucretius » Sat Oct 25, 2008 6:35 pm This seems to fit Rei's motivation in ep. 23 or 26' more than in episode 6. I mean, she's barely had any human contact at all when she says "I will protect you." Her attitude towards Shinji at first seems to be one of apathy mixed with obligation, until the smiling bit at the end of the episode, where she begins to warm up to him.

[]: Re: A Translation From Beyond the Grave             Quote Post by pat457 » Sat Oct 25, 2008 7:56 pm NAveryW wrote: Zuggy asked me to post this on EGF since he was unable to. I'm hoping this is the right subforum... In any case, it's a poem written by Megumi Hayashibara and published in ＥＶＡ友の会. (snip)

[]:             Quote Post by V » Sat Oct 25, 2008 8:29 pm he got it from the old LaserDisk liner comics

[]:             Quote Post by Zerogoki » Sun Oct 26, 2008 3:27 pm Lucretius wrote: I mean, she's barely had any human contact at all when she says "I will protect you."

[]:             Quote Post by The Goose » Sun Oct 26, 2008 4:46 pm I'm confused about what the poem means and what it has to do with the show.

[]:             Quote Post by AyrYntake » Mon Oct 27, 2008 8:31 am pat457 wrote: Is there any raw version of this floating in the 'net somewhere?

[]:             Quote Post by Lucretius » Mon Oct 27, 2008 8:40 am The Goose wrote: I'm confused about what the poem means and what it has to do with the show.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Mon Oct 27, 2008 12:53 pm V: Zuggy is the one who translated this, added that comment, not NAveryW. Anyway, that poem is interesting with shedding some light on Rei. I guess this also partially explains why she (well, Rei 3) chooses Shinji over Gendo later on. For her own happiness, since even though Rei wants to die, she wants it to be on her terms.

[]:             Quote Post by Shay Guy » Sat Nov 22, 2008 11:08 pm ...How the hell does she find the time for POETRY, too?!
